heading: Return of foreign excess property to United States
label: 702 Return of foreign excess property to United States
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)70240:512(c)., title IV, § 402(c), as added , , ; , , .June 30, 1949, ch. 288Pub. L. 91–426, § 2(d)84 Stat. 883Pub. L. 94–519, § 490 Stat. 2455Sept. 26, 1970Oct. 17, 1976
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)70240:512(c)., title IV, § 402(c), as added , , ; , , .June 30, 1949, ch. 288Pub. L. 91–426, § 2(d)84 Stat. 883Pub. L. 94–519, § 490 Stat. 2455Sept. 26, 1970Oct. 17, 1976

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Under regulations prescribed pursuant to subsection (b), foreign excess property may be returned to the United States for handling as excess or surplus property under subchapter II of chapter 5 of this title or section 549 or 551 of this title when the head of the executive agency concerned, or the Administrator of General Services after consultation with the agency head, determines that return of the property to the United States for such handling is in the interest of the United States.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: b .—Regulations
children.text: The Administrator shall prescribe regulations to carry out this section. The regulations must require that transportation costs for returning foreign excess property to the United States are paid by the federal agency, state agency, or donee receiving the property.
